{"text": "Discussion in 'Mac OS X Lion (10.7)' started by axboi87, Jan 20, 2012.\nI've got a 500gb internal drive and a 240gb SSD.\nWhen trying to restore using
 disk utility i'm given the error \"Not enough space on disk ____ to restore\"\nBut I shouldn't have to do that!!!\nAny ideas or workarounds before resorting to
 the above?\nUse Carbon Copy Cloner to copy one drive to the other. I've done this several times going from larger HDD to smaller SSD and I wound up with a boot
able SSD drive. One step you have to remember not to skip is to use Disk Utility to partition the SSD as GUID partition scheme HFS+ before doing the clone. If i
t came Apple Partition Scheme, even if you let CCC do the clone, the resulting drive won't be bootable. CCC usually works in \"file mode\" and it can easily cop
y a larger drive (that's mostly empty) onto a smaller drive. If you tell CCC to clone a drive you did NOT boot from, it can work in block copy mode where the de
stination drive must be the same size or larger than the drive you are cloning from (if I recall).\nI've actually done this somehow on Disk Utility several time
s (booting from a different drive (or even the dvd) so not running disk utility from the drive your cloning) and had it work just fine from larger to smaller bo
otable clone. Definitely format the drive cloning to first, as bootable Apple etc..\nThanks for pointing this out. My only experience using DU to go larger to s
maller was when I was trying to make a Lion install stick and I was unable to restore InstallESD.dmg to a 4 GB USB stick but of course the reason that wouldn't
fit is there was slightly more than 4 GB of data.", "url": "https://forums.macrumors.com/threads/restore-from-larger-disk-to-smaller-disk.1311329/"}

action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "Good! The pyproject.toml file has been created. Now I need to create the compress.py script that will handle resharding the data according to the constraints: max 30 files per directory, max 15MB per file, and create metadata for reconstruction.",
  "plan": "I'll create compress.py first. It needs to: 1) Take input and output directories as arguments, 2) Create the output directory if it doesn't exist, 3) Organize files into subdirectories with max 30 files each, 4) Split files larger than 15MB while preserving JSONL structure, 5) Create metadata for reconstruction. Then I'll create decompress.py to reverse the process.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > compress.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import os
import sys
import json
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

def split_large_file(file_path, max_size_mb=15):
    """Split a file if it's larger than max_size_mb MB, preserving JSONL structure."""
    max_size_bytes = max_size_mb * 1024 * 1024
    file_size = os.path.getsize(file_path)
    
    if file_size <= max_size_bytes:
        return [file_path]  # No splitting needed
    
    parts = []
    base_name = file_path.stem
    extension = file_path.suffix
    parent_dir = file_path.parent
    
    with open(file_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as infile:
        part_num = 0
        current_size = 0
        current_lines = []
        
        for line in infile:
            line_size = len(line.encode('utf-8'))
            
            # If adding this line would exceed the limit, save current part
            if current_size + line_size > max_size_bytes and current_lines:
                part_filename = f"{base_name}_part_{part_num:03d}{extension}"
                part_path = parent_dir / part_filename
                
                with open(part_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as outfile:
                    for current_line in current_lines:
                        outfile.write(current_line)
                
                parts.append(part_path)
                part_num += 1
                current_lines = []
                current_size = 0
            
            current_lines.append(line)
            current_size += line_size
        
        # Save the last part if there are remaining lines
        if current_lines:
            part_filename = f"{base_name}_part_{part_num:03d}{extension}"
            part_path = parent_dir / part_filename
            
            with open(part_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as outfile:
                for current_line in current_lines:
                    outfile.write(current_line)
            
            parts.append(part_path)
    
    return parts

def compress_directory(input_dir, output_dir):
    """Compress directory according to resharding constraints."""
    input_path = Path(input_dir)
    output_path = Path(output_dir)
    
    if not input_path.exists():
        print(f"Error: Input directory {input_dir} does not exist")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    # Create output directory if it doesn't exist
    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    
    # Collect all files from input directory
    all_files = []
    if input_path.is_file():
        all_files = [input_path]
    else:
        for root, dirs, files in os.walk(input_path):
            for file in files:
                file_path = Path(root) / file
                all_files.append(file_path)
    
    # Create metadata for reconstruction
    metadata = {
        "original_structure": {},
        "split_files": {},
        "directories": []
    }
    
    # Process files and handle splitting
    processed_files = []
    for file_path in all_files:
        relative_path = file_path.relative_to(input_path) if input_path.is_dir() else file_path.name
        
        # Copy file to temp location and split if necessary
        temp_file = output_path / "temp" / file_path.name
        temp_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        shutil.copy2(file_path, temp_file)
        
        # Split if file is too large
        split_parts = split_large_file(temp_file)
        
        if len(split_parts) > 1:
            # File was split
            metadata["split_files"][str(relative_path)] = [part.name for part in split_parts]
            processed_files.extend(split_parts)
        else:
            # File was not split
            metadata["original_structure"][str(relative_path)] = temp_file.name
            processed_files.extend(split_parts)
    
    # Organize files into subdirectories with max 30 files each
    files_per_dir = 30
    dir_count = 0
    
    for i in range(0, len(processed_files), files_per_dir):
        batch = processed_files[i:i + files_per_dir]
        subdir = output_path / f"shard_{dir_count:04d}"
        subdir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        metadata["directories"].append(f"shard_{dir_count:04d}")
        
        for file_path in batch:
            dest_path = subdir / file_path.name
            shutil.move(str(file_path), str(dest_path))
        
        dir_count += 1
    
    # Save metadata
    with open(output_path / "_metadata.json", 'w') as f:
        json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)
    
    # Clean up temp directory
    temp_dir = output_path / "temp"
    if temp_dir.exists():
        shutil.rmtree(temp_dir)
    
    print(f"Compression complete. Created {dir_count} shards in {output_dir}")

def main():
    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
        print("Usage: python /app/compress.py <input_dir> <output_dir>")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    input_dir = sys.argv[1]
    output_dir = sys.argv[2]
    
    compress_directory(input_dir, output_dir)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}

root@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app# cat > decompress.py << 'EOF'
> #!/usr/bin/env python3
>
> import os
> import sys
> import json
> import shutil
> from pathlib import Path
>
> def reconstruct_split_files(resharded_dir, metadata):
>     """Reconstruct files that were split during compression."""
>     resharded_path = Path(resharded_dir)
>
>     for original_file, part_names in metadata["split_files"].items():
>         # Find all parts across shards
>         all_parts = []
>         for part_name in part_names:
>             for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>                 part_path = resharded_path / shard_dir / part_name
>                 if part_path.exists():
>                     all_parts.append(part_path)
>                     break
>
>         # Sort parts by their part number
>         all_parts.sort(key=lambda x: x.name)
>
>         # Reconstruct the original file
>         original_name = Path(original_file).name
>         # Remove the _part_XXX suffix to get original name
>         if "_part_" in original_name:
>             base_name = original_name.split("_part_")[0]
>             extension = Path(original_name).suffix
>             reconstructed_name = base_name + extension
>         else:
>             reconstructed_name = original_name
>
>         reconstructed_path = resharded_path / reconstructed_name
>
>         # Concatenate all parts
>         with open(reconstructed_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as outfile:
>             for part_path in all_parts:
>                 with open(part_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as infile:
>                     outfile.write(infile.read())
>
>         # Remove the part files
>         for part_path in all_parts:
>             part_path.unlink()
>
> def move_files_to_root(resharded_dir, metadata):
>     """Move all files from shards back to the root directory."""
>     resharded_path = Path(resharded_dir)
>
>     # Move files that weren't split
>     for original_file, current_name in metadata["original_structure"].items():
>         # Find the file in the shards
>         for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>             file_path = resharded_path / shard_dir / current_name
>             if file_path.exists():
>                 dest_path = resharded_path / Path(original_file).name
>                 shutil.move(str(file_path), str(dest_path))
>                 break
>
>     # Move any remaining files (parts of split files will be handled separately)
>     for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>         shard_path = resharded_path / shard_dir
>         if shard_path.exists():
>             for file_path in shard_path.iterdir():
>                 if file_path.is_file():
>                     dest_path = resharded_path / file_path.name
>                     if not dest_path.exists():  # Avoid conflicts
>                         shutil.move(str(file_path), str(dest_path))
>
> def decompress_directory(resharded_dir):
>     """Decompress a resharded directory back to original structure."""
>     resharded_path = Path(resharded_dir)
>
>     if not resharded_path.exists():
>         print(f"Error: Resharded directory {resharded_dir} does not exist")
>         sys.exit(1)
>
>     metadata_path = resharded_path / "_metadata.json"
>     if not metadata_path.exists():
>         print(f"Error: Metadata file not found in {resharded_dir}")
>         sys.exit(1)
>
>     # Load metadata
>     with open(metadata_path, 'r') as f:
>         metadata = json.load(f)
>
>     # Move files from shards to root
>     move_files_to_root(resharded_dir, metadata)
>
>     # Reconstruct split files
>     reconstruct_split_files(resharded_dir, metadata)
>
>     # Remove empty shard directories
>     for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>         shard_path = resharded_path / shard_dir
>         if shard_path.exists():
>             try:
>                 shard_path.rmdir()  # Only removes if empty
>             except OSError:
>                 # Directory not empty, remove remaining files first
>                 shutil.rmtree(shard_path)
>
>     # Remove metadata file
>     metadata_path.unlink()
>
>     print(f"Decompression complete. Original structure restored in {resharded_dir}")
>

> #!/usr/bin/env python3

>
/> def reconstruct_split_files(resharded_dir, metadata):
>     """Reconstruct files that were split during compression."""
>     for original_file, part_names in metadata["split_files"].items():
>         # Find all parts across shards
>         all_parts = []
>         for part_name in part_names:
>             for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>                 part_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, shard_dir, part_name)
>                 if os.path.exists(part_path):
>                     all_parts.append(part_path)
>                     break
>
>         # Sort parts by their part number
>         all_parts.sort(key=lambda x: os.path.basename(x))
>
>         # Reconstruct the original file
>         original_name = os.path.basename(original_file)
>         # Remove the _part_XXX suffix to get original name
>         if "_part_" in original_name:
>             base_name = original_name.split("_part_")[0]
>             extension = os.path.splitext(original_name)[1]
>             reconstructed_name = base_name + extension
>         else:
>             reconstructed_name = original_name
>
>         reconstructed_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, reconstructed_name)
>
>         # Concatenate all parts
>         with open(reconstructed_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as outfile:
>             for part_path in all_parts:
>                 with open(part_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as infile:
>                     outfile.write(infile.read())
>
>         # Remove the part files
>         for part_path in all_parts:
>             os.unlink(part_path)
>
> def move_files_to_root(resharded_dir, metadata):
>     """Move all files from shards back to the root directory."""
>     # Move files that weren't split
>     for original_file, current_name in metadata["original_structure"].items():
>         # Find the file in the shards
>         for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>             file_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, shard_dir, current_name)
>             if os.path.exists(file_path):
>                 dest_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, os.path.basename(original_file))
>                 shutil.move(file_path, dest_path)
>                 break
>
>     # Move any remaining files (parts of split files will be handled separately)
>     for shard_dir in metadata["directories"]:
>         shard_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, shard_dir)
>         if os.path.exists(shard_path):
>             for filename in os.listdir(shard_path):
>                 file_path = os.path.join(shard_path, filename)
>                 if os.path.isfile(file_path):
>                     dest_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, filename)
>                     if not os.path.exists(dest_path):  # Avoid conflicts
>                         shutil.move(file_path, dest_path)
>
> def decompress_directory(resharded_dir):
>     """Decompress a resharded directory back to original structure."""
>     if not os.path.exists(resharded_dir):
>         print(f"Error: Resharded directory {resharded_dir} does not exist")
>         sys.exit(1)
>
>     metadata_path = os.path.join(resharded_dir, "_metadata.json")

>         sys.exit(1)
>
>     # Load metadata

>

>
